summary_quote: Aim alignment: 2/10. Scope: on-scope. Verdict: rescope. Pipeline regresses 3 of 4 stated success criteria — no seeds, no split, no per-epoch metrics file, no test final-accuracy. Nothing it produces can be trusted.

findings.title: Pipeline regresses three of four stated success criteria; nothing it produces is trustworthy by the user's own bar
findings.explanation: Aim alignment: 2/10. Scope: on-scope. Per-criterion: (a) 'Training is reproducible' — REGRESSED. No torch.manual_seed, no numpy.random.seed, DataLoader has no worker_init_fn, TabularDataset uses unseeded np.random at init. Two runs produce different loss curves by construction. (b) 'Train/val/test split, no leakage' — REGRESSED. load_full_dataset() returns a single unsplit dataset; train() iterates the full set. Every reported metric is a training-set metric. (c) 'Metrics logged per epoch' — PARTIAL. Training loss printed but no val loss, no val accuracy, no persistent log artifact. (d) 'Test set accuracy number at end' — NOT TOUCHED. train.py prints 'training complete' and exits; predict() exists but is never called. The fix is roughly 55 lines across three changes.
findings.suggestion: Block-and-rescope. Three surgical changes close the gap: (1) Add seeding at top of train() — torch.manual_seed(cfg['seed']), numpy seed, worker_init_fn for DataLoader; ~15 lines. (2) Replace load_full_dataset() with load_splits() returning (train_ds, val_ds, test_ds) via deterministic index split; log val loss and val accuracy per epoch; ~30 lines. (3) After the epoch loop, run test-set pass with model.eval() and log the single accuracy number; ~10 lines. After these, every Stage 2 ML finding maps to a green criterion.

findings.title: Performance findings are off-aim relative to the missing reproducibility and split work
findings.explanation: team-performance-reviewer's findings (DataLoader num_workers=0, zero_grad without set_to_none, per-call numpy->tensor conversion) are real hygiene issues but carry low alignment weight here. For a 32-dim, 10k-sample, 20-epoch MLP the under-30-minute constraint is met regardless of these optimizations. Shipping perf fixes before reproducibility means faster runs of an untrustworthy pipeline.
findings.suggestion: Sequence: (1) reproducibility + split + final test accuracy; (2) per-epoch metrics persistence as a structured artifact; (3) defer DataLoader and perf nits to a follow-up unless the 30-min constraint is actually breached.

stage_handoff_notes: Aims are explicit and well-captured; no rescoping of aims.md is needed. The 'rescope' verdict means the PR contents need to expand to cover the stated criteria, not that user intent has drifted. peer-quality-engineer's block on test coverage is consistent — once splitter and seeding land, the criterion-a 'identical loss curves' check is itself the natural first regression test. team-security-reviewer's medium on unvalidated cfg_path is not weighted in the alignment grade — aims.md does not name security as a criterion for this local-only ml-pipeline.
